title: Incarceration rate: US vs global benchmarks (per 100,000 people)

source.name: World Prison Brief
source.publisher: Institute for Crime & Justice Policy Research, Birkbeck, University of London

notes: Illustrative; the US incarcerates more people per capita than any other democracy. The world average is calculated across ~220 jurisdictions. Western Europe averages roughly 100 per 100,000.
